architecture_fit.note: No attention or transformer at all; pure NumPy cumsum that re-implements the generator, so it never addresses whether attention can compute a range sum.

baseline_comparison.note: Beats the built-in constant-predictor baseline trivially (exact targets), but constructs no attention-vs-no-attention strawman of its own.

faithfulness.note: No model exists to test; it reconstructs ground-truth sums directly, so there is no mechanism to causally ablate.

operating_range.note: Sweeps k=2..32 with 0 MSE and robustness 1.0, but the invariance is trivial because the arithmetic is exact rather than learned.

hardcoded_weights_bonus.note: Hand-built rather than learned, but it bypasses attention entirely instead of hand-setting an attention circuit, so it shows no mechanism understanding.

visual_judgement.note: Demo tab only emits a 'Run loaded successfully' status string; no chart or interactive view of the claim, just the generic benchmark panel.

visualisation_rationale.note: README argues no dynamic demo is needed since MSE is trivially zero; weak, since it skips visualising any actual mechanism.

notes: This attempt scores perfect MSE only because it re-implements the dataset generator's own cumsum to reconstruct the targets; it contains no attention head, no transformer, and no model whatsoever. It therefore fails the goal's central question (can attention express a range sum) on architecture fit and faithfulness, and the empty Demo tab adds no interpretive value. Useful only as a declared upper-bound reference, not as a mechanistic result.
